evals.prompt: Audit the AI-native readiness of this ML.NET repository
evals.expected_output: A structured audit report showing L4 or L5 level with scores per layer, since ML.NET has a complete AI-native setup
expectations: The audit checked for AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and copilot-instructions.md
The audit checked for agent files in .github/agents/
The audit checked for prompt files in .github/prompts/
The audit checked for copilot-setup-steps.yml
The output includes a score table with Foundation, Skills, Agents, Orchestration, and Automation layers
The overall level is L4 or L5 (ML.NET has a complete setup)
The output includes a gap analysis section
The output includes prioritized recommendations

evals.prompt: Audit a repo that only has a README.md and source code — no AI-native files at all
evals.expected_output: Report showing L0 with all layers scored at 0 and recommendations to start with foundation layer
expectations: The overall level is L0 (Unaware)
All layer scores are 0 or near 0
The gap analysis lists AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and copilot-instructions.md as missing
The first recommendation is to create the foundation layer files
The audit does NOT give credit for files that don't exist

evals.prompt: Audit a repo that has copilot-instructions.md with correct build commands but nothing else
evals.expected_output: Report showing L1-partial with foundation partially complete, all other layers at 0
expectations: The overall level is L1
Foundation layer has a partial score (not 0, not full)
Skills, Agents, Orchestration, Automation layers are all 0
The audit verified that the build commands in copilot-instructions.md are correct
Recommendations start with completing the foundation layer

evals.prompt: Audit a repo that has all the files but they contain generic placeholder content like {{PROJECT_NAME}}
evals.expected_output: Report showing low quality scores despite all files existing, flagging generic content
expectations: The audit detected that files contain placeholder/generic content
Quality scores are low despite files existing
The gap analysis specifically mentions generic content as a deficiency
Recommendations include customizing files with repo-specific patterns
